metadata.title: Firefly Network — Lights in the Dark
metadata.description: A surveillance-resistant network for collective intelligence. Anonymous but human. Verified but private. Distributed but connected.

hero.title: Lights in the Dark
hero.subtitle: A surveillance-resistant network for collective intelligence. Where verified humans share, corroborate, and build understanding — without exposing their identities.

whatIs.title: What is the Firefly Network?
whatIs.p1: In the Caribbean, indigenous peoples used cocuyos — bioluminescent beetles — as natural lamps to guide them through the dark. No single cocuyo illuminates a path. But millions of tiny sparks, together, can light an entire nation.
whatIs.p2: The Firefly Network is not a social media platform. It is a collective intelligence network where every participant is both sensor and analyst, where information spreads through corroboration rather than amplification, and where the value of a contribution is determined by the cryptographic weight of human verification — not algorithms or engagement metrics.
whatIs.p3: Here, you are not a user. You are a firefly — a verified human being contributing light to the collective understanding.

illuminate.title: Illuminate
illuminate.description: Share what you observe. A post is an observation, a piece of evidence, a data point. Each post is signed by your anonymous credential, proving a verified human created it.

corroborate.title: Corroborate
corroborate.description: Verify what others share. Corroboration is not a like — it's a reputation-staked act. When you corroborate, you're putting your accumulated reputation behind your assessment.

connect.title: Connect
connect.description: Posts link into story chains — emergent structures that form as fireflies connect their observations. The chain becomes stronger than any individual post through distributed verification.

whatIsNot.title: What This Is Not
whatIsNot.noFollowers: No follower counts. No profiles. No celebrities. Fireflies are anonymous.
whatIsNot.noScroll: No infinite scroll. No algorithmic feed. No engagement optimization.
whatIsNot.noLikes: No likes, reactions, or shares. Information spreads through corroboration, not amplification.
whatIsNot.noTracking: No data collection. No tracking. No surveillance. We don't collect what we can't be forced to reveal.

polkadot.title: Built on Polkadot
polkadot.description: The Firefly Network is built on and for the Polkadot ecosystem — leveraging decentralized identity, censorship-resistant storage, and cross-chain interoperability. No single point of failure. No single point of control.

hero.title: About the Network
hero.subtitle: Millions of tiny sparks can illuminate an entire nation.

fireflyPrinciple.title: The Firefly Principle
fireflyPrinciple.p1: In the Caribbean, indigenous peoples used cocuyos — bioluminescent beetles — as natural lamps to guide them through the dark. A single cocuyo casts barely enough light to see your own hand. But gather them together, and they become something more.
fireflyPrinciple.p2: This is the principle behind the Firefly Network: no single observer sees the whole picture. But when verified humans share what they observe, corroborate what others see, and connect their posts into chains of evidence — the darkness begins to lift.
fireflyPrinciple.p3: We are inspired by Efecto Cocuyo, a Venezuelan independent media platform founded in 2015 by journalists who refused to let government censorship silence the truth. They proved that community-driven journalism could survive and thrive even under authoritarian conditions. The firefly image resonated because it speaks to something universal: small lights that together illuminate the darkness.

anonymousButHuman.title: Anonymous but Human
anonymousButHuman.description: Every firefly is a verified human being — proved through DIM (Decentralized Identity Mechanism), a proof-of-personhood system that confirms you're human without revealing who you are. No usernames, no profiles, no personal data. Just a cryptographic credential that says: "A real human made this."

verifiedButPrivate.title: Verified but Private
verifiedButPrivate.description: Every post and corroboration carries cryptographic proof. Every chain has a transparent verification trail. But none of this requires exposing anyone's identity. The verification is public; the verifier is private. This is fact-checking without surveillance.

distributedButConnected.title: Distributed but Connected
distributedButConnected.description: Posts and chains are stored on decentralized infrastructure. No central server to seize, no company to subpoena, no single point of failure. If the founding team disappeared tomorrow, the network would continue. That's the point.

posts.title: Posts
posts.description: A post is an observation, a piece of evidence, a witness account, a data point. When you illuminate a post, you're contributing a discrete piece of information — signed by your anonymous credential, tagged with optional context (topic, location, timeframe), and ready to connect with other posts.

corroboration.title: Corroboration
corroboration.description: Corroboration is not a like. It's a reputation-staked act of verification. When you corroborate a post, you're making a specific claim: "I can independently confirm this," or "I have additional evidence," or "This is consistent with my expertise." Your corroboration is recorded permanently, weighted by your reputation in the relevant domain. If the post is later debunked, your reputation diminishes.

storyChains.title: Story Chains
storyChains.description: Story chains are emergent structures. No one "creates" a chain — chains crystallize as fireflies link their posts to related observations. A chain about water quality might start with one person noticing a smell, grow as others corroborate and add photos, and strengthen as someone contributes public records about permits. The chain becomes more than the sum of its posts.

verificationTrails.title: Verification Trails
verificationTrails.description: Every chain carries a transparent verification trail — the complete history of posts, corroborations, challenges, and evidence. You don't need to trust us to tell you something is verified. You can trace the trail yourself and see exactly how many independent humans, with what reputation, made what claims.

campaigns.title: Campaigns
campaigns.description: Campaigns are sponsored fact-checking efforts. News outlets, collectives, or community members fund a request for specific information — and fireflies who contribute valuable evidence earn a share of the reward. The funds are held in escrow until deliverables are met. Payments can be public (pUSD) or private (Coinage) to protect contributor identity. This creates a functioning market for investigative journalism.

surveillanceResistance.title: Surveillance Resistance
surveillanceResistance.intro: The network is designed so that no single point of compromise — no server seizure, no subpoena, no insider threat — can expose the identity of any firefly or remove any post from the network.
surveillanceResistance.noUserDatabase: No user database. DIM credentials are generated through proof-of-personhood and held by you. There is no central registry of participants.
surveillanceResistance.noContentServer: No content server. Posts are stored on decentralized infrastructure. There is no single server to seize.
surveillanceResistance.noMetadata: No metadata collection. We don't log IP addresses, device fingerprints, or access patterns.
surveillanceResistance.noCookies: No cookies. None. Zero. We don't collect what we can't be forced to reveal.

builtOnPolkadot.title: Built on Polkadot
builtOnPolkadot.intro: The Firefly Network is built on and for the Polkadot ecosystem. We chose Polkadot because its values align with ours: decentralization, interoperability, and user sovereignty.
builtOnPolkadot.parachains: Polkadot parachains — Verification and reputation logic

openSource.title: Open Source
openSource.description: All code is open source. All governance is public. All verification trails are auditable. Transparency is not optional — it's foundational.

explore.title: Stories
explore.description: Browse ongoing investigations and ground-truth from the network.
explore.noStories: No stories yet. Illuminate a post to start one.

storiesInfo.title: What are Stories?
storiesInfo.body:
Story chains are emergent structures. No one "creates" a story — stories crystallize as fireflies link their posts to related observations.

Some stories have campaigns attached (marked with "Earn $X"). These are sponsored fact-checking efforts — contribute valuable evidence and earn a share of the reward.

The story becomes more than the sum of its posts.

postsInfo.title: What are Posts?
postsInfo.body:
A post is an observation, a piece of evidence, a witness account, a data point.

When you illuminate a post, you're contributing information signed by your anonymous credential — ready to connect with other posts and form story chains.

Posts gain weight through corroboration from other verified humans.

openCampaignsInfo.title: What are Open Campaigns?
openCampaignsInfo.body:
Campaigns are sponsored fact-checking efforts funded by news outlets, collectives, or community members who need specific information verified.

The funds are held in escrow until campaign deliverables are met. When you contribute valuable evidence, you earn a share of the reward.

Payments can be public (pUSD) or private (Coinage) to protect your identity. Open campaigns are waiting for contributors — be the first to illuminate.

campaigns.title: Campaigns
campaigns.description: Sponsored fact-checking efforts. Contribute evidence to earn rewards.

info.title: What are Campaigns?
info.body:
Campaigns are sponsored fact-checking efforts funded by news outlets, collectives, or community members. When you have evidence that supports a campaign, illuminate a post and link it.

If your post contributes valuable information, you earn a share of the campaign reward. Payments can be made publicly via pUSD or privately via Coinage.

Campaigns create a functioning market for investigative journalism — funded by those who need verified information.
